steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Left: independent clause ending with “set any art curator's mind into [noun]”
Right: verb form used as a noun (“finding”) plus its object, elaborating on that noun

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content: A colon introduces an elaboration that clarifies what precedes it — “motion: finding that thread” shows what the motion is about. A pairs comma and colon awkwardly, B creates a fragment after a period, C uses a semicolon which requires independent clauses on both sides. This matches choice D — D is correct.
